service_coverage.rationale: REGISTRY_KV is a derived hourly snapshot of approved public Worker metadata and is rebuilt from source-owned runtime configuration.
service_coverage.notes: Loss causes a temporary registry warm-up until the next collection pass; it does not remove an authoritative source record.

service_coverage.rationale: A synthetic KV-shaped export preserves deterministic restore validation for the public API storage adapter.
service_coverage.notes: Fixture evidence does not prove a live production KV backup exists.

service_coverage.rationale: Fixture targets exercise artifact metadata and incident export validation without production access.
service_coverage.notes: Fixture evidence does not prove a live backup exists.

service_coverage.rationale: A disposable Chroma-shaped export validates local restore mechanics.
service_coverage.notes: Fixture evidence does not prove the live corpus is backed up.

service_coverage.rationale: The digest Worker owns no declared mutable store; each run is regenerated from upstream sources and reviewed Git configuration.
service_coverage.notes: Credentials, upstream availability, and Home Assistant handoff recovery remain separate operating concerns.

service_coverage.rationale: The static viewer is rebuilt from reviewed Git source and has no declared mutable service-owned store.
service_coverage.notes: Provider deployment recovery remains separate from data backup.

service_coverage.rationale: DORA_CACHE contains derived aggregates and correlation results that are recreated from the bounded public evidence sources.
service_coverage.notes: Loss invalidates cache warmth only; source evidence remains owned by atlas-notify, atlas-blackbox, deploy-watch, and atlas-api-public.

service_coverage.rationale: The rolling event buffer contains non-reconstructible operational context consumed by incident and delivery evidence, so the owner approved a six-hour recovery point objective.
service_coverage.notes: The fixture validates the KV restore shape only; it does not prove that production NOTIFY_LOG is currently exported.

service_coverage.rationale: The service owns no declared mutable store; quota and cost evidence is recomputed from provider reads and policy configuration.
service_coverage.notes: Provider credentials and external billing history remain outside this service's backup boundary.

service_coverage.rationale: The static Pages output is deterministically rebuilt from reviewed Git source; mutable provider data is not part of the declared service contract.
service_coverage.notes: Git history and deployment recovery remain outside this fixture audit.

service_coverage.rationale: DEPLOY_STATE stores the latest Pages deployment observation and is re-derived from Cloudflare on the next scheduled pass.
service_coverage.notes: Loss removes only the cached latest-state snapshot, not the provider deployment history.

service_coverage.rationale: PULSE_CACHE contains derived GitHub activity responses and workflow evidence that can be repopulated from GitHub.
service_coverage.notes: The GitHub source remains authoritative; cache loss is acceptable.

service_coverage.rationale: The edge gateway is stateless and rebuilt from reviewed Git source; private upstream state is owned outside this service.
service_coverage.notes: Credential rotation and upstream recovery are separate runbooks.

service_coverage.rationale: Long-term conversation memory is persistent Chroma data that cannot be reconstructed from Git, so the owner approved a 24-hour recovery point objective.
service_coverage.notes: The fixture validates the Chroma export and disposable restore shape only; it does not prove a live ramone-memory backup exists.

service_coverage.rationale: The trigger Worker is stateless and rebuilt from reviewed Git source; workflow execution history is owned by GitHub.
service_coverage.notes: No trigger credential or workflow state is read by this audit.

service_coverage.rationale: PULSE_CACHE contains derived Cloudflare Analytics responses and is repopulated from the provider on the next refresh.
service_coverage.notes: Analytics source data remains provider-owned; local cache loss is acceptable.

service_coverage.rationale: The edge projection is stateless and rebuilt from reviewed Git source; telemetry history belongs to the local producer.
service_coverage.notes: This rationale does not cover local telemetry data.

service_coverage.rationale: The only persisted local state is the previously delivered WSL2 address, which is re-derived from the current network state after recovery.
service_coverage.notes: The edge stores delivered health reports; the sentinel itself owns no irreplaceable history.

service_coverage.rationale: The telemetry-to-audio Worker is stateless and rebuilt from reviewed Git source.
service_coverage.notes: Its input telemetry is owned by the source service.

service_coverage.rationale: The local anomaly state is bounded detector history and baseline evidence that is relearned from fresh hardware telemetry after restart.
service_coverage.notes: With the default 24-sample window and 30-second cadence, detector warm-up is about 36 minutes; loss does not remove source telemetry or reviewed configuration.

service_coverage.rationale: The status site is static and rebuilt from reviewed Git source; no mutable service-owned store is declared.
service_coverage.notes: Provider deployment recovery remains separate from data backup.

targets.notes: Validates a synthetic NOTIFY_LOG-shaped export without reading or writing the production namespace.
Validates a small Chroma-shaped export without reading production data.
Validates a synthetic KV export adapter without reading or writing a Cloudflare namespace.
Validates artifact metadata only; no GitHub artifact is downloaded.
Validates a synthetic incident export without reading a Durable Object or private incident.
Validates a synthetic ramone_memory Chroma export without reading the live local vector store.
